Trie

Last updated: Sep 2, 2025

Trie

Whenever we come across questions with multiple strings, it is best to think if Trie can help us.

  • https://leetcode.com/problems/search-suggestions-system/solution/

0) Concept

  • https://blog.csdn.net/fuxuemingzhu/article/details/79388432
  • tree + dict
    • put Node into dict (e.g. defaultdict(Node))

0-1) Types

0-2) Pattern

1class TrieNode:
2    def __init__(self):
3        self.children = {}  # HashMap for flexible alphabet
4        self.is_end = False
5        self.word = None    # Store complete word (optional)
6
7class Trie:
8    def __init__(self):
9        self.root = TrieNode()
10    
11    def insert(self, word: str) -> None:
12        """Insert word into trie. Time: O(m), Space: O(m)"""
13        node = self.root
14        for char in word:
15            if char not in node.children:
16                node.children[char] = TrieNode()
17            node = node.children[char]
18        node.is_end = True
19        node.word = word  # Store for easy retrieval
20    
21    def search(self, word: str) -> bool:
22        """Search for exact word. Time: O(m)"""
23        node = self.root
24        for char in word:
25            if char not in node.children:
26                return False
27            node = node.children[char]
28        return node.is_end
29    
30    def startsWith(self, prefix: str) -> bool:
31        """Check if any word starts with prefix. Time: O(p)"""
32        node = self.root
33        for char in prefix:
34            if char not in node.children:
35                return False
36            node = node.children[char]
37        return True
38
39# Java version
40class TrieNode {
41    Map<Character, TrieNode> children;
42    boolean isEnd;
43    String word;
44    
45    public TrieNode() {
46        children = new HashMap<>();
47        isEnd = false;
48        word = null;
49    }
50}
51
52class Trie {
53    private TrieNode root;
54    
55    public Trie() {
56        root = new TrieNode();
57    }
58    
59    public void insert(String word) {
60        TrieNode node = root;
61        for (char c : word.toCharArray()) {
62            node.children.putIfAbsent(c, new TrieNode());
63            node = node.children.get(c);
64        }
65        node.isEnd = true;
66        node.word = word;
67    }
68    
69    public boolean search(String word) {
70        TrieNode node = root;
71        for (char c : word.toCharArray()) {
72            if (!node.children.containsKey(c)) {
73                return false;
74            }
75            node = node.children.get(c);
76        }
77        return node.isEnd;
78    }
79    
80    public boolean startsWith(String prefix) {
81        TrieNode node = root;
82        for (char c : prefix.toCharArray()) {
83            if (!node.children.containsKey(c)) {
84                return false;
85            }
86            node = node.children.get(c);
87        }
88        return true;
89    }
90}

Template 2: Trie with Array (Fixed Alphabet)

1class TrieNode:
2    def __init__(self):
3        self.children = [None] * 26  # For lowercase letters only
4        self.is_end = False
5
6class Trie:
7    def __init__(self):
8        self.root = TrieNode()
9    
10    def insert(self, word: str) -> None:
11        node = self.root
12        for char in word:
13            idx = ord(char) - ord('a')
14            if not node.children[idx]:
15                node.children[idx] = TrieNode()
16            node = node.children[idx]
17        node.is_end = True
18    
19    def search(self, word: str) -> bool:
20        node = self._search_prefix(word)
21        return node is not None and node.is_end
22    
23    def startsWith(self, prefix: str) -> bool:
24        return self._search_prefix(prefix) is not None
25    
26    def _search_prefix(self, prefix: str) -> TrieNode:
27        node = self.root
28        for char in prefix:
29            idx = ord(char) - ord('a')
30            if not node.children[idx]:
31                return None
32            node = node.children[idx]
33        return node
34
35# Java version
36class TrieNode {
37    TrieNode[] children;
38    boolean isEnd;
39    
40    public TrieNode() {
41        children = new TrieNode[26];
42        isEnd = false;
43    }
44}

Template 3: Trie with Wildcard Support

1class WildcardTrie:
2    def __init__(self):
3        self.root = TrieNode()
4    
5    def insert(self, word: str) -> None:
6        node = self.root
7        for char in word:
8            if char not in node.children:
9                node.children[char] = TrieNode()
10            node = node.children[char]
11        node.is_end = True
12    
13    def search(self, word: str) -> bool:
14        """Search with '.' as wildcard for any character"""
15        return self._dfs_search(word, 0, self.root)
16    
17    def _dfs_search(self, word: str, index: int, node: TrieNode) -> bool:
18        if index == len(word):
19            return node.is_end
20        
21        char = word[index]
22        if char == '.':
23            # Try all possible children
24            for child in node.children.values():
25                if self._dfs_search(word, index + 1, child):
26                    return True
27            return False
28        else:
29            if char not in node.children:
30                return False
31            return self._dfs_search(word, index + 1, node.children[char])
32
33# Java version
34public boolean search(String word) {
35    return dfsSearch(word, 0, root);
36}
37
38private boolean dfsSearch(String word, int index, TrieNode node) {
39    if (index == word.length()) {
40        return node.isEnd;
41    }
42    
43    char c = word.charAt(index);
44    if (c == '.') {
45        for (TrieNode child : node.children.values()) {
46            if (dfsSearch(word, index + 1, child)) {
47                return true;
48            }
49        }
50        return false;
51    } else {
52        if (!node.children.containsKey(c)) {
53            return false;
54        }
55        return dfsSearch(word, index + 1, node.children.get(c));
56    }
57}

Template 4: Autocomplete Trie

1class AutocompleteTrie:
2    def __init__(self):
3        self.root = TrieNode()
4    
5    def insert(self, word: str) -> None:
6        node = self.root
7        for char in word:
8            if char not in node.children:
9                node.children[char] = TrieNode()
10            node = node.children[char]
11        node.is_end = True
12        node.word = word
13    
14    def search_prefix(self, prefix: str, limit: int = 3) -> List[str]:
15        """Return up to 'limit' words with given prefix"""
16        node = self.root
17        
18        # Navigate to prefix end
19        for char in prefix:
20            if char not in node.children:
21                return []
22            node = node.children[char]
23        
24        # Collect all words with this prefix
25        results = []
26        self._dfs_collect(node, results, limit)
27        return results
28    
29    def _dfs_collect(self, node: TrieNode, results: List[str], limit: int):
30        if len(results) >= limit:
31            return
32        
33        if node.is_end:
34            results.append(node.word)
35        
36        # Traverse in lexicographical order
37        for char in sorted(node.children.keys()):
38            self._dfs_collect(node.children[char], results, limit)
39
40# Java version with priority queue for top suggestions
41class AutocompleteTrie {
42    class TrieNode {
43        Map<Character, TrieNode> children = new HashMap<>();
44        Map<String, Integer> counts = new HashMap<>();  // Word -> frequency
45    }
46    
47    public List<String> getTopSuggestions(String prefix, int k) {
48        TrieNode node = root;
49        for (char c : prefix.toCharArray()) {
50            if (!node.children.containsKey(c)) {
51                return new ArrayList<>();
52            }
53            node = node.children.get(c);
54        }
55        
56        // Use heap to get top k
57        PriorityQueue<Map.Entry<String, Integer>> pq = new PriorityQueue<>(
58            (a, b) -> b.getValue() - a.getValue()
59        );
60        pq.addAll(node.counts.entrySet());
61        
62        List<String> result = new ArrayList<>();
63        while (!pq.isEmpty() && result.size() < k) {
64            result.add(pq.poll().getKey());
65        }
66        return result;
67    }
68}

Template 5: Binary Trie (XOR Problems)

1class BinaryTrie:
2    class Node:
3        def __init__(self):
4            self.children = [None, None]  # 0 and 1
5            self.count = 0
6    
7    def __init__(self):
8        self.root = self.Node()
9    
10    def insert(self, num: int) -> None:
11        """Insert number as 32-bit binary"""
12        node = self.root
13        for i in range(31, -1, -1):
14            bit = (num >> i) & 1
15            if not node.children[bit]:
16                node.children[bit] = self.Node()
17            node = node.children[bit]
18            node.count += 1
19    
20    def find_max_xor(self, num: int) -> int:
21        """Find maximum XOR with num"""
22        node = self.root
23        max_xor = 0
24        
25        for i in range(31, -1, -1):
26            bit = (num >> i) & 1
27            # Try to go opposite direction for max XOR
28            toggled = 1 - bit
29            
30            if node.children[toggled] and node.children[toggled].count > 0:
31                max_xor |= (1 << i)
32                node = node.children[toggled]
33            else:
34                node = node.children[bit]
35        
36        return max_xor
37    
38    def remove(self, num: int) -> None:
39        """Remove number from trie"""
40        node = self.root
41        for i in range(31, -1, -1):
42            bit = (num >> i) & 1
43            node = node.children[bit]
44            node.count -= 1
45
46# Java version
47class BinaryTrie {
48    class Node {
49        Node[] children = new Node[2];
50        int count = 0;
51    }
52    
53    private Node root = new Node();
54    
55    public void insert(int num) {
56        Node node = root;
57        for (int i = 31; i >= 0; i--) {
58            int bit = (num >> i) & 1;
59            if (node.children[bit] == null) {
60                node.children[bit] = new Node();
61            }
62            node = node.children[bit];
63            node.count++;
64        }
65    }
66    
67    public int findMaxXor(int num) {
68        Node node = root;
69        int maxXor = 0;
70        
71        for (int i = 31; i >= 0; i--) {
72            int bit = (num >> i) & 1;
73            int toggled = 1 - bit;
74            
75            if (node.children[toggled] != null && node.children[toggled].count > 0) {
76                maxXor |= (1 << i);
77                node = node.children[toggled];
78            } else {
79                node = node.children[bit];
80            }
81        }
82        
83        return maxXor;
84    }
85}

Template 6: Trie with Delete Operation

1// java
2// LC 208 (Modified)
3    // V0
4    // IDEA : https://github.com/yennanliu/CS_basics/blob/master/leetcode_python/Tree/implement-trie-prefix-tree.py#L49
5    // modified by GPT
6    class TrieNode {
7
8        /** NOTE !!!!
9         *
10         *  Define children as map structure:
11         *
12         *   Map<String, TrieNode>
13         *
14         *  -> string : current "element"
15         *  -> TrieNode : the child object
16         *
17         */
18        Map<String, TrieNode> children;
19        boolean isWord;
20
21        public TrieNode() {
22            children = new HashMap<>();
23            isWord = false;
24        }
25    }
26
27    /**
28     *  NOTE !!!
29     *
30     *  Define 2 classes
31     *
32     *   1) TrieNode
33     *   2) Trie
34     *
35     */
36    class Trie {
37        TrieNode root;
38
39        public Trie() {
40
41            root = new TrieNode();
42        }
43
44        public void insert(String word) {
45            /**
46             *  NOTE !!! get current node first
47             */
48            TrieNode cur = root;
49            for (String c : word.split("")) {
50                cur.children.putIfAbsent(c, new TrieNode());
51                // move node to its child
52                cur = cur.children.get(c);
53            }
54            cur.isWord = true;
55        }
56
57        public boolean search(String word) {
58            /**
59             *  NOTE !!! get current node first
60             */
61            TrieNode cur = root;
62            for (String c : word.split("")) {
63                cur = cur.children.get(c);
64                if (cur == null) {
65                    return false;
66                }
67            }
68            return cur.isWord;
69        }
70
71        public boolean startsWith(String prefix) {
72            /**
73             *  NOTE !!! get current node first
74             */
75            TrieNode cur = root;
76            for (String c : prefix.split("")) {
77                cur = cur.children.get(c);
78                if (cur == null) {
79                    return false;
80                }
81            }
82            return true;
83        }
84    }

1# python
2
3#--------------------------
4# V1
5#--------------------------
6
7from collections import defaultdict
8### NOTE : need to define our Node
9class Node():
10
11    def __init__(self):
12        """
13        NOTE : we use defaultdict(Node) as our trie data structure
14
15        -> use defaultdict
16            - key : every character from word
17            - value : Node (Node type)
18
19        and link children with parent Node via defaultdict
20        """
21        self.children = defaultdict(Node)
22        self.isword = False
23
24# implement basic methods
25class Trie():
26
27    def __init__(self):
28        self.root = Node()
29
30    def insert(self, word):
31        ### NOTE : we always start from below
32        cur = self.root
33        for w in word:
34            cur = cur.children[w] # same as self.root.defaultdict[w]
35        cur.isword = True
36
37    def search(self, word):
38        ### NOTE : we always start from below
39        cur = self.root
40        for w in word:
41            cur = cur.children.get(w)
42            if not cur:
43                return False
44        ### NOTE : need to check if isword
45        return cur.isword
46
47    def startsWith(self, prefix):
48        ### NOTE : we always start from below
49        cur = self.root
50        for p in prefix:
51            cur = cur.children.get(p)
52            if not cur:
53                return False
54        return True

1) General form

1-1) Basic OP

2) LC Example

2-1) Implement Trie (Prefix Tree)

1# 208. Implement Trie (Prefix Tree)
2# V0
3# IDEA : trie concept :  dict + tree
4# https://blog.csdn.net/fuxuemingzhu/article/details/79388432
5### NOTE : we need implement Node class
6from collections import defaultdict
7class Node(object):
8    def __init__(self):
9        ### NOTE : we use defaultdict as dict
10        # TODO : make a default py dict version
11        self.children = defaultdict(Node)
12        self.isword = False
13        
14class Trie(object):
15
16    def __init__(self):
17        """
18        Initialize your data structure here.
19        """
20        ### NOTE : we use the Node class we implement above
21        self.root = Node()
22
23    def insert(self, word):
24        current = self.root
25        for w in word:
26            current = current.children[w]
27        ### NOTE : if insert OP completed, mark isword attr as true
28        current.isword = True
29
30    def search(self, word):
31        current = self.root
32        for w in word:
33            current = current.children.get(w)
34            if current == None:
35                return False
36        ### NOTE : we need to check if isword atts is true (check if word terminated here as well)
37        return current.isword
38
39    def startsWith(self, prefix):
40        current = self.root
41        for w in prefix:
42            current = current.children.get(w)
43            if current == None:
44                return False
45        ### NOTE : we don't need to check isword here, since it is "startsWith"
46        return True

1// java
2// LC 208
3// V1
4// https://leetcode.com/problems/implement-trie-prefix-tree/editorial/
5class TrieNode {
6
7    // R links to node children
8    private TrieNode[] links;
9
10    private final int R = 26;
11
12    private boolean isEnd;
13
14    public TrieNode() {
15        links = new TrieNode[R];
16    }
17
18    public boolean containsKey(char ch) {
19        return links[ch -'a'] != null;
20    }
21    public TrieNode get(char ch) {
22        return links[ch -'a'];
23    }
24    public void put(char ch, TrieNode node) {
25        links[ch -'a'] = node;
26    }
27    public void setEnd() {
28        isEnd = true;
29    }
30    public boolean isEnd() {
31        return isEnd;
32    }
33}
34
35class Trie2 {
36    private TrieNode root;
37
38    public Trie2() {
39        root = new TrieNode();
40    }
41
42    // Inserts a word into the trie.
43    public void insert(String word) {
44        TrieNode node = root;
45        for (int i = 0; i < word.length(); i++) {
46            char currentChar = word.charAt(i);
47            if (!node.containsKey(currentChar)) {
48                node.put(currentChar, new TrieNode());
49            }
50            node = node.get(currentChar);
51        }
52        node.setEnd();
53    }
54
55
56    // search a prefix or whole key in trie and
57    // returns the node where search ends
58    private TrieNode searchPrefix(String word) {
59        TrieNode node = root;
60        for (int i = 0; i < word.length(); i++) {
61            char curLetter = word.charAt(i);
62            if (node.containsKey(curLetter)) {
63                node = node.get(curLetter);
64            } else {
65                return null;
66            }
67        }
68        return node;
69    }
70
71    // Returns if the word is in the trie.
72    public boolean search(String word) {
73        TrieNode node = searchPrefix(word);
74        return node != null && node.isEnd();
75    }
76
77    // Returns if there is any word in the trie
78    // that starts with the given prefix.
79    public boolean startsWith(String prefix) {
80        TrieNode node = searchPrefix(prefix);
81        return node != null;
82    }
83
84}

2-2) Add and Search Word - Data structure design

1# LC 211 Add and Search Word - Data structure design
2# V0
3from collections import defaultdict
4class Node(object):
5    def __init__(self):
6        self.children = defaultdict(Node)
7        self.isword = False
8        
9class WordDictionary(object):
10
11    def __init__(self):
12        self.root = Node()
13
14    def addWord(self, word):
15        current = self.root
16        for w in word:
17            _next = current.children[w]
18            current = _next
19        current.isword = True
20
21    def search(self, word):
22        return self.match(word, 0, self.root)
23    
24    def match(self, word, index, root):
25        """
26        NOTE : match is a helper func (for search)
27
28          - deal with 2 cases
29          -   1) word[index] != '.'
30          -   2) word[index] == '.'
31        """
32        # note the edge cases
33        if root == None:
34            return False
35        if index == len(word):
36            return root.isword
37        # CASE 1: word[index] != '.'
38        if word[index] != '.':
39            return root != None and self.match(word, index + 1, root.children.get(word[index]))
40        # CASE 2: word[index] == '.'
41        else:
42            for child in root.children.values():
43                if self.match(word, index + 1, child):
44                    return True
45        return False

1// java
2// LC 211
3
4// V1
5// IDEA : TRIE
6// https://leetcode.com/problems/design-add-and-search-words-data-structure/editorial/
7class TrieNode {
8    Map<Character, TrieNode> children = new HashMap();
9    boolean word = false;
10    public TrieNode() {}
11}
12
13class WordDictionary2 {
14    TrieNode trie;
15
16    /** Initialize your data structure here. */
17    public WordDictionary2() {
18        trie = new TrieNode();
19    }
20
21    /** Adds a word into the data structure. */
22    public void addWord(String word) {
23        TrieNode node = trie;
24
25        for (char ch : word.toCharArray()) {
26            if (!node.children.containsKey(ch)) {
27                node.children.put(ch, new TrieNode());
28            }
29            node = node.children.get(ch);
30        }
31        node.word = true;
32    }
33
34    /** Returns if the word is in the node. */
35    public boolean searchInNode(String word, TrieNode node) {
36        for (int i = 0; i < word.length(); ++i) {
37            char ch = word.charAt(i);
38            if (!node.children.containsKey(ch)) {
39                // if the current character is '.'
40                // check all possible nodes at this level
41                if (ch == '.') {
42                    for (char x : node.children.keySet()) {
43                        TrieNode child = node.children.get(x);
44                        /** NOTE !!!
45                         *  -> if ".", we HAVE to go through all nodes in next levels
46                         *  -> and check if any of them is valid
47                         *  -> so we need to RECURSIVELY call searchInNode method with "i+1" sub string
48                         */
49                        if (searchInNode(word.substring(i + 1), child)) {
50                            return true;
51                        }
52                    }
53                }
54                // if no nodes lead to answer
55                // or the current character != '.'
56                return false;
57            } else {
58                // if the character is found
59                // go down to the next level in trie
60                node = node.children.get(ch);
61            }
62        }
63        return node.word;
64    }
65
66    /** Returns if the word is in the data structure. A word could contain the dot character '.' to represent any one letter. */
67    public boolean search(String word) {
68        return searchInNode(word, trie);
69    }
70}

2-3) Search Suggestions System

1# LC 1268. Search Suggestions System
2# V1
3# IDEA : TRIE
4# https://leetcode.com/problems/search-suggestions-system/discuss/436183/Python-Trie-Solution
5class TrieNode:
6    def __init__(self):
7        self.children = dict()
8        self.words = []
9
10class Trie:
11    def __init__(self):
12        self.root = TrieNode()
13    
14    def insert(self, word):
15        node = self.root
16        for char in word:
17            if char not in node.children:
18                node.children[char] = TrieNode()
19            node = node.children[char]
20            node.words.append(word)
21            node.words.sort()
22            while len(node.words) > 3:
23                node.words.pop()
24    
25    def search(self, word):
26        res = []
27        node = self.root
28        for char in word:
29            if char not in node.children:
30                break
31            node = node.children[char]
32            res.append(node.words[:])
33        l_remain = len(word) - len(res)
34        for _ in range(l_remain):
35            res.append([])
36        return res
37
38class Solution:
39    def suggestedProducts(self, products: List[str], searchWord: str):
40        trie = Trie()
41        for prod in products:
42            trie.insert(prod)
43        return trie.search(searchWord)

1// IDEA : trie + dfs
2// https://leetcode.com/problems/search-suggestions-system/solution/
3// Custom class Trie with function to get 3 words starting with given prefix
4class Trie {
5
6    // Node definition of a trie
7    class Node {
8        boolean isWord = false;
9        List<Node> children = Arrays.asList(new Node[26]);
10    };
11    Node Root, curr;
12    List<String> resultBuffer;
13
14    // Runs a DFS on trie starting with given prefix and adds all the words in the resultBuffer, limiting result size to 3
15    void dfsWithPrefix(Node curr, String word) {
16        if (resultBuffer.size() == 3)
17            return;
18        if (curr.isWord)
19            resultBuffer.add(word);
20
21        // Run DFS on all possible paths.
22        for (char c = 'a'; c <= 'z'; c++)
23            if (curr.children.get(c - 'a') != null)
24                dfsWithPrefix(curr.children.get(c - 'a'), word + c);
25    }
26    Trie() {
27        Root = new Node();
28    }
29
30    // Inserts the string in trie.
31    void insert(String s) {
32
33        // Points curr to the root of trie.
34        curr = Root;
35        for (char c : s.toCharArray()) {
36            if (curr.children.get(c - 'a') == null)
37                curr.children.set(c - 'a', new Node());
38            curr = curr.children.get(c - 'a');
39        }
40
41        // Mark this node as a completed word.
42        curr.isWord = true;
43    }
44    List<String> getWordsStartingWith(String prefix) {
45        curr = Root;
46        resultBuffer = new ArrayList<String>();
47        // Move curr to the end of prefix in its trie representation.
48        for (char c : prefix.toCharArray()) {
49            if (curr.children.get(c - 'a') == null)
50                return resultBuffer;
51            curr = curr.children.get(c - 'a');
52        }
53        dfsWithPrefix(curr, prefix);
54        return resultBuffer;
55    }
56};
57class Solution {
58    List<List<String>> suggestedProducts(String[] products,
59                                         String searchWord) {
60        Trie trie = new Trie();
61        List<List<String>> result = new ArrayList<>();
62        // Add all words to trie.
63        for (String w : products)
64            trie.insert(w);
65        String prefix = new String();
66        for (char c : searchWord.toCharArray()) {
67            prefix += c;
68            result.add(trie.getWordsStartingWith(prefix));
69        }
70        return result;
71    }
72};

2-4) Word Search

1# LC 79. Word Search
2# V0
3# IDEA : DFS + backtracking
4class Solution(object):
5 
6    def exist(self, board, word):
7        ### NOTE : construct the visited matrix
8        visited = [[False for j in range(len(board[0]))] for i in range(len(board))]
9
10        """
11        NOTE !!!! : we visit every element in board and trigger the dfs
12        """
13        for i in range(len(board)):
14            for j in range(len(board[0])):
15                if self.dfs(board, word, 0, i, j, visited):
16                    return True
17
18        return False
19
20    def dfs(self, board, word, cur, i, j, visited):
21        # if "not false" till cur == len(word), means we already found the wprd in board
22        if cur == len(word):
23            return True
24
25        ### NOTE this condition
26        # 1) if idx out of range
27        # 2) if already visited
28        # 3) if board[i][j] != word[cur] -> not possible to be as same as word
29        if i < 0 or i >= len(board) or j < 0 or j >= len(board[0]) or visited[i][j] or board[i][j] != word[cur]:
30            return False
31
32        # NOTE THIS !! : mark as visited
33        visited[i][j] = True
34        ### NOTE THIS TRICK (run the existRecu on 4 directions on the same time)
35        result = self.dfs(board, word, cur + 1, i + 1, j, visited) or\
36                 self.dfs(board, word, cur + 1, i - 1, j, visited) or\
37                 self.dfs(board, word, cur + 1, i, j + 1, visited) or\
38                 self.dfs(board, word, cur + 1, i, j - 1, visited)
39        # mark as non-visited
40        visited[i][j] = False
41
42        return result

2-5) Word Search II

1# LC 212. Word Search II
2
3# V0
4# IDEA : DFS + trie
5# DEMO
6# >>> words = ['oath', 'pea', 'eat', 'rain'], trie = {'o': {'a': {'t': {'h': {'#': None}}}}, 'p': {'e': {'a': {'#': None}}}, 'e': {'a': {'t': {'#': None}}}, 'r': {'a': {'i': {'n': {'#': None}}}}}
7class Solution(object):
8    def checkList(self, board, row, col, word, trie, rList):
9        if row<0 or row>=len(board) or col<0 or col>=len(board[0]) or board[row][col] == '.' or board[row][col] not in trie:
10            return
11        c = board[row][col]
12        _word= word + c
13        if '#' in trie[c]: 
14            rList.add(_word)
15            if len(trie[c]) == 1:
16                return # if next node is empty, return as no there is no need to search further
17        board[row][col] = '.'
18        self.checkList(board, row-1, col, _word, trie[c], rList) #up
19        self.checkList(board, row+1, col, _word, trie[c], rList) #down
20        self.checkList(board, row, col-1, _word, trie[c], rList) #left
21        self.checkList(board, row, col+1, _word, trie[c], rList) #right
22        board[row][col] = c
23    
24    def findWords(self, board, words):
25        if not board or not words:
26            return []
27        # building Trie
28        trie, rList = {}, set()
29        for word in words:
30            t = trie
31            for c in word:
32                if c not in t:
33                    t[c] = {}
34                t = t[c]
35            t['#'] = None
36        #print (">>> words = {}, trie = {}".format(words, trie))
37        for row in range(len(board)):
38            for col in range(len(board[0])):
39                if board[row][col] in trie:
40                    self.checkList(board, row, col, "", trie, rList)
41        return list(rList)
42
43# V1
44# IDEA : Backtracking with Trie
45# https://leetcode.com/problems/word-search-ii/solution/
46class Solution:
47    def findWords(self, board, words):
48        WORD_KEY = '$'
49        
50        trie = {}
51        for word in words:
52            node = trie
53            for letter in word:
54                # retrieve the next node; If not found, create a empty node.
55                node = node.setdefault(letter, {})
56            # mark the existence of a word in trie node
57            node[WORD_KEY] = word
58        
59        rowNum = len(board)
60        colNum = len(board[0])
61        
62        matchedWords = []
63        
64        def backtracking(row, col, parent):    
65            
66            letter = board[row][col]
67            currNode = parent[letter]
68            
69            # check if we find a match of word
70            word_match = currNode.pop(WORD_KEY, False)
71            if word_match:
72                # also we removed the matched word to avoid duplicates,
73                #   as well as avoiding using set() for results.
74                matchedWords.append(word_match)
75            
76            # Before the EXPLORATION, mark the cell as visited 
77            board[row][col] = '#'
78            
79            # Explore the neighbors in 4 directions, i.e. up, right, down, left
80            for (rowOffset, colOffset) in [(-1, 0), (0, 1), (1, 0), (0, -1)]:
81                newRow, newCol = row + rowOffset, col + colOffset     
82                if newRow < 0 or newRow >= rowNum or newCol < 0 or newCol >= colNum:
83                    continue
84                if not board[newRow][newCol] in currNode:
85                    continue
86                backtracking(newRow, newCol, currNode)
87        
88            # End of EXPLORATION, we restore the cell
89            board[row][col] = letter
90        
91            # Optimization: incrementally remove the matched leaf node in Trie.
92            if not currNode:
93                parent.pop(letter)
94
95        for row in range(rowNum):
96            for col in range(colNum):
97                # starting from each of the cells
98                if board[row][col] in trie:
99                    backtracking(row, col, trie)
100        
101        return matchedWords
102
103# V1'
104# IDEA : DFS + trie
105# https://leetcode.com/problems/word-search-ii/discuss/59808/Python-DFS-362ms
106class Solution(object):
107    def checkList(self, board, row, col, word, trie, rList):
108        if row<0 or row>=len(board) or col<0 or col>=len(board[0]) or board[row][col] == '.' or board[row][col] not in trie: return
109        c = board[row][col]
110        _word= word + c
111        if '#' in trie[c]: 
112            rList.add(_word)
113            if len(trie[c]) == 1: return # if next node is empty, return as no there is no need to search further
114        board[row][col] = '.'
115        self.checkList(board, row-1, col, _word, trie[c], rList) #up
116        self.checkList(board, row+1, col, _word, trie[c], rList) #down
117        self.checkList(board, row, col-1, _word, trie[c], rList) #left
118        self.checkList(board, row, col+1, _word, trie[c], rList) #right
119        board[row][col] = c
120    
121    def findWords(self, board, words):
122        """
123        :type board: List[List[str]]
124        :type words: List[str]
125        :rtype: List[str]
126        """
127        if not board or not words: return []
128        # building Trie
129        trie, rList = {}, set()
130        for word in words:
131            t = trie
132            for c in word:
133                if c not in t: t[c] = {}
134                t = t[c]
135            t['#'] = None
136        for row in range(len(board)):
137            for col in range(len(board[0])):
138                if board[row][col] not in trie: continue
139                self.checkList(board, row, col, "", trie, rList)
140        return list(rList)

CS_basics - Computer Science Fundamentals & Interview Preparation

GitHub • Documentation • Report Issues
